name: sms
context: Send and receive SMS messages using the Twilio Messaging API
info: This skill covers sending and receiving SMS and MMS messages through the Twilio Messaging API. It handles outbound message creation, inbound webhook configuration, delivery status callbacks, MMS media attachments, and Messaging Services for high-volume sending. The primary interface is the `client.messages.create()` method in the Twilio Node.js helper library. Messages are sent from a Twilio phone number or Messaging Service SID to any E.164-formatted destination number. Status callbacks provide real-time delivery tracking.

sections.title: Sending SMS
sections.body: Use `client.messages.create({ to, from, body })` to send an SMS. The `to` number must be in E.164 format (`+1XXXXXXXXXX`). The `from` must be a Twilio phone number you own or a Messaging Service SID. The response includes `messageSid`, `status` (initially `queued`), and `dateCreated`. See [[twilio#phone-numbers]] for purchasing numbers.

sections.title: Receiving SMS
sections.body: Configure a webhook URL on your Twilio phone number. Twilio POSTs inbound messages with `From`, `To`, `Body`, `NumMedia`, and `MessageSid`. Respond with TwiML to reply automatically (see [[twilio/twiml#messaging-verbs]]). Use request validation to verify that webhooks originate from Twilio.

sections.title: Status callbacks
sections.body: Set `statusCallback` on message creation to receive delivery updates. Twilio POSTs status changes: `queued`, `sent`, `delivered`, `failed`, `undelivered`. The callback includes `MessageSid`, `MessageStatus`, `ErrorCode` (if failed), and `To`. Use this to track delivery rates and retry failed messages.

sections.title: MMS media
sections.body: Include `mediaUrl` (array of URLs) to send MMS with images, GIFs, audio, or video. Twilio fetches the media from the URL and attaches it. Maximum 10 media items per message. MMS is supported on US/Canadian long codes and short codes. For international, use WhatsApp (see [[twilio/whatsapp#media]]).

sections.title: Messaging Services
sections.body: Messaging Services pool multiple phone numbers for high-volume sending. They handle sender selection, opt-out management (`STOP`/`START`), sticky sender (same number to same recipient), and compliance. Create a service in the Console, add numbers to its pool, and use `messagingServiceSid` instead of `from` in message creation.

scripts.name: Send SMS
scripts.description: Send an SMS message to a phone number using the Twilio Messaging API

TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER.description: Twilio phone number to send from (E.164 format)

to.description: Destination phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567)

body.description: Text content of the SMS message

from.description: Twilio phone number or Messaging Service SID to send from

mediaUrl.description: Array of media URLs to attach as MMS

messageSid.description: Unique identifier for the message (starts with SM)

status.description: Message status (queued, sent, delivered, failed, undelivered)

depends.reason: Requires Twilio account credentials and a phone number
